stories.headline: Spot BTC ETFs pull $1.7B in April as Strategy stacks 56,238 BTC for $4.1B
stories.tldr: Spot Bitcoin ETFs logged about $1.7B in April net inflows, the largest monthly haul since October 2025, while Strategy added 56,238 BTC.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Institutional bid is absorbing supply even as price chops sideways, narrowing float ahead of any May break.
the_details: April spot BTC ETF net inflows of approximately $1.7 billion mark the heaviest monthly print since October 2025.
Strategy (MSTR) bought 56,238 BTC worth roughly $4.1 billion during the month, per filings cited by Bitget Research.
Total crypto market cap rose ~10% in April to about $2.7T, with BTC up 16% and ETH up 14%.

stories.headline: DeFi bleeds $606M across 12 exploits in 18 days as Kelp DAO contagion lingers
stories.tldr: April losses across DeFi protocols hit $606M in 18 days, headlined by Kelp DAO's $293M bridge drain and Drift's $285M April 1 exploit.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Two North Korea-linked attacks in a single month are forcing Aave, SparkLend and Fluid to freeze markets and rethink cross-chain LRT collateral.
the_details: Kelp DAO lost ~$293M on April 19 after a misconfigured LayerZero bridge let an attacker mint 116,500 unbacked rsETH across 20 chains.
Drift Protocol was drained for $285M on April 1 via pre-signed Solana 'durable nonces' authorizations, attributed to Trader Traitor.
Smaller hits: Dango bridge aggregator lost $410K; Silo Finance bled $392K on April 3 from a misconfigured oracle.

stories.headline: BTC closes April defensive at $76K as 30-year yield hits 5% and oil rips
stories.tldr: Bitcoin opened April 30 at $75,752 — its lowest open in over a week — before recovering to $76,130 as yields and Iran-tied oil pressure risk assets.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A 50/100-day moving average bullish crossover is loading, but rising bond yields and a 4-year-high BTC-DXY inverse correlation set up a binary May.
the_details: BTC opened $75,752.12 on April 30, the lowest weekly open; ETH opened $2,252.45, lowest since April 13.
U.S. 30-year Treasury yield touched 5%, weighing on long-duration risk assets including crypto.
CryptoQuant flags rising profit-taking risk as exchange inflows jump back near 76K cycle highs.

stories.headline: Senate market-structure bill markup slips to May as 100+ firms pile pressure
stories.tldr: Sen. Tillis confirmed the Senate Banking Committee will not mark up the crypto market-structure bill in April, pushing the vote into May.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The slip extends regulatory limbo for U.S. exchanges and DeFi, but a 100+ firm coalition led by Coinbase and Ripple is now openly lobbying for a May markup.
the_details: Sen. Thom Tillis (R-N.C.) said the Banking Committee will not hold an April amend-and-vote hearing on the bill.
A coalition of 100+ crypto firms, including Coinbase and Ripple, sent letters urging Senate action on the Clarity Act framework.
Slip pushes legislative timing closer to summer recess, raising risk that 2026 passage stalls into Q4.

stories.headline: Solana Alpenglow upgrade targets 150ms finality — 80x faster than today
stories.tldr: Solana's pending Alpenglow consensus overhaul (SIMD-0326) aims to cut block finality from ~12 seconds to roughly 150 milliseconds by moving validator votes off-chain.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Sub-second finality would put Solana ahead of every L1 on settlement speed and unlock HFT-grade DeFi just as RWA value on the chain crossed $1.85B.
the_details: SIMD-0326 (Alpenglow) compresses finality from ~12s to ~150ms by relocating validator vote processing off-chain.
Solana RWA value surpassed $1.85B and stablecoin supply on the chain hit $17B in April.
DoubleZero Edge launched real-time Solana data feeds on April 16 to complement the latency push for traders.
